赛马会幼儿「喜步」计划: Hong Kong Jockey Club - Bright Start
GOVHK香港政府一站通亲职教育: GOVHK Hong Kong Government One-stop Parent Education
HOME1_CONTENT01: The Hong Kong Jockey Club Bright Start Programme Phase 2 (2025-2029) aims to promote quality infant and toddler care and education concepts to families with children aged 0-3 across Hong Kong and the broader community, creating a comprehensive quality environment that promotes healthy growth and development of infants and toddlers.
HOME1_CONTENT02: This programme is funded by the Hong Kong Jockey Club Charities Trust, organized by Yew Chung College of Early Childhood Education, with the Pacific Early Childhood Education Research Association (Hong Kong) and The Chinese University of Hong Kong as professional partners.
HOME1_TITLE: Quality Care and Education, Bright Start Future
HOME2_CONTENT01: The Hong Kong Jockey Club Bright Start Programme Phase 2 is a 4-year programme (2025-2029) that continues to receive support from the Hong Kong Jockey Club Charities Trust, with funding of approximately HK$200 million. The programme will cover approximately 80% of childcare centres in Hong Kong that provide services for newborns to 3-year-olds, benefiting 15 participating childcare centres, other kindergartens and childcare centres, major social service units, related parents and community members.
HOME2_CONTENT02: This programme is organized by Yew Chung College of Early Childhood Education, with the Pacific Early Childhood Education Research Association (Hong Kong) and The Chinese University of Hong Kong as professional partners. Through cross-professional collaboration, it provides professional guidance on care and education practices for participating childcare centres, and further promotes parent education and community participation support, creating a favorable environment for infant and toddler development.
HOME2_CONTENT03: This phase of the programme will cover a series of professional support projects, including: cross-professional support and collaboration, establishing professional learning communities, overseas professional exchanges, parent and community practices, infrastructure enhancement and digital platforms, to promote continuous optimization of infant and toddler care and education.
HOME2_CONTENT04: "Bright Start" is the beginning of life, allowing infants and toddlers to enjoy the highest quality care and education services to enrich their future. This is the intention of the Hong Kong Jockey Club Bright Start Programme. We hope to work together with cross-professional practitioners, parents and community partners to create a new chapter for the future of early childhood education in Hong Kong, in response to society's demand for quality early childhood education services.
HOME3_CONTENT01: The Hong Kong Jockey Club Bright Start Programme will cover approximately 80% of childcare centres in Hong Kong that provide services for newborns to 3-year-olds, benefiting 15 participating childcare centres, other kindergartens and childcare centres, major social service units, related parents and community members. This phase of the programme will cover a series of professional support projects, including: cross-professional support and collaboration, establishing professional learning communities, overseas professional exchanges, parent and community practices, infrastructure enhancement and digital platforms, to promote continuous optimization of infant and toddler care and education.
HOME4_CONTENT01: Enhance Professional Competence of Early Childhood Teachers
HOME4_CONTENT03: Improve Quality of Infant and Toddler Services
HOME4_EXPLAIN: This programme benefits 15 participating childcare centres, other kindergartens and childcare centres, major social service units, related parents and community members.
HOME5_CONTENT01: Knowledge Corridor: Professional Video Series on Basic Early Childhood Education
